question_text: A student drops a rubber ball from a height of 2 metres. Just before it hits the ground, most of the energy has been transferred from which store to which store?

options.label: A
options.text: From kinetic store to gravitational potential store

options.label: B
options.text: From gravitational potential store to kinetic store

options.label: C
options.text: From elastic store to thermal store

options.label: D
options.text: From chemical store to kinetic store

explanation: As the ball falls, it loses height, so gravitational potential energy decreases. At the same time, the ball speeds up, so kinetic energy increases. The energy is transferred from the gravitational potential store to the kinetic store.
